description: Per-user priority contact list. Recipients on this list are classified as Priority when tracking sent emails, triggering the shortest follow-up delay (default 1 business day). The Sent Items Tracker flow checks this table during recipient classification.

alternateKeys.purpose: Ensures one priority contact entry per email per user. Enables safe Upsert when adding contacts. (250 + 36) × 2 = 572 bytes, within SQL Server's 900-byte index limit.

columns.description: Email address of the priority contact. Used for lookup in Flow 1 during recipient classification and in Flow 4 for sender priority checking. Stored lowercase for case-insensitive matching.
Display name of the priority contact. Optional — used for Canvas App display and nudge card priority badges. If not provided, the email address is shown.

The Azure AD object ID of the owning user (from Office 365 Users connector body/id). Part of composite alternate key. Uses cr_owneruserid instead of ownerid because Dataverse does not support ownerid in alternate keys.
Optional notes about why this contact is priority (e.g., 'Executive sponsor', 'Key client stakeholder'). Displayed in the Canvas App priority contacts management screen.

notes.cardinality: Multiple rows per user, one row per priority contact. A user with 10 priority contacts has 10 rows.
notes.lookup_pattern: Flow 1 queries the full list for the current user once per flow run, then uses Filter Array to check each recipient against the cached list. This avoids N+1 queries inside the recipient loop.
notes.sender_check: Flow 4 (Auto-Unsnooze) queries this table to check if the reply sender is a priority contact. If so, IS_PRIORITY_SENDER=true is passed to the Snooze Agent, which defaults to UNSNOOZE with High urgency.
notes.security: UserOwned table with ownership-based RLS. Users can only read/write their own priority contacts.
notes.system_columns: Standard Dataverse system columns (ownerid, createdon, modifiedon, statecode, statuscode) are created automatically.
